title: Yacht Hire in Marbella: Costs, Seasons and What to Know
html:
Yacht hire in Marbella puts the Costa del Sol within easy reach, with most charters leaving from Puerto Banus and a day price band of roughly EUR 600 to EUR 3,000 depending on the boat. BoatHire24 lists verified yachts so you can match crew, cabins and budget before you book.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most yachts depart from a small cluster of well-known harbours along this stretch of the southern Spanish coast. The right base shapes your day, since each marina sits a short hop from a different beach.

  • Puerto Banus: the glamorous main marina and the usual jumping-off point for a day at sea.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the older harbour beside the old town, handy for an early start.
  • Puerto de Cabopino: a quieter marina to the east, close to the Artola dunes.
  • La Bajadilla: Marbella's fishing port, a practical pick-up point away from the crowds.
  • Estepona: a short cruise west, with its own marina and a long sandy shore.
  • Benalmadena: further east towards Malaga, useful for longer coastal runs.

Which yacht to rent in Marbella?

The right yacht depends on your group size and whether you want speed, shade or stability for a day at anchor. Marbella charters span nippy motorboats to crewed flybridge yachts, so weigh comfort against cost before you commit. Below are the most popular choices on this coast.

  1. Small motorboat: a short self-drive coastal spin, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 200 a half-day.
  2. Flybridge motor yacht: sun, shade and a higher helm view, seats around 8 guests, from EUR 900 a day.
  3. Sailing catamaran: stable cruising and easy swimming, seats up to 10 guests, from EUR 1,200 a day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht: a hands-off day with a skipper, seats up to 10 guests, from EUR 1,800 a day.
  5. Sailing yacht: a quieter, wind-driven cruise, seats up to 8 guests, from EUR 700 a day.

For a fast, sociable flybridge on a Banus day trip, it is worth taking a moment to compare the Fairline Targa 43, a sporty motor yacht that handles the local swell well.

How does booking a yacht in Marbella work?

You pick a boat, confirm the date and crew option, then pay a deposit to secure it, usually weeks ahead in summer. Peak weekends sell out fast, so enquire early.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own, and each listing sets out capacity, fuel rules and skipper options clearly. If you are new to chartering, the guide on how boat rental works covers deposits, what is included and the marina handover.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Marbella?

Prices in 2026 vary widely with boat size, season and whether a skipper is included. The bands below give a realistic guide in euros for a day around Marbella.

  • A shared group tour from Puerto Banus typically costs from EUR 45 per person.
  • A small self-drive motorboat for a half-day starts at around EUR 200 in EUR.
  • A sailing catamaran for a full day runs from roughly EUR 1,200.
  • A fully crewed yacht with skipper generally starts near EUR 1,800 per day.

To gauge a fair rate, it helps to compare verified boat listings side by side, since published day prices reveal what is genuinely on offer.

When is the best season to hire a yacht in Marbella?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm, settled days and clear water make for the best cruising. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and noticeably cheaper.

The defining local wind is the Levante, an easterly that can build choppy seas, while the milder Poniente blows from the west. Spanish rules let you hire many smaller boats without a personal licence when a skipper is provided, though the principle varies by boat size and the operator confirms exact requirements before you sail.

Local tip: book your Marbella charter for a weekday in late June or early September, when the water is warm, the Levante tends to ease and Puerto Banus is far less crowded than at the August peak.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 600 to EUR 3,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: a full day of 6 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: up to 10 on most day yachts
  • Currency: EUR
  • Sample distance: around 14 nautical miles west to Estepona

Questions about yacht rental in Marbella

Do I need a licence to hire a yacht in Marbella?

For most crewed charters you do not need a personal licence, because a qualified skipper handles the boat. If you want to drive yourself, the operator will confirm what certification applies to that specific vessel.

How many people fit on a Marbella yacht?

Day yachts on this coast typically carry up to 10 guests, while smaller motorboats seat around 6. Larger crewed yachts can take more, so always check the listed capacity for your chosen boat.

How far can you cruise in a day from Marbella?

A relaxed day trip often covers the local beaches and bays, but you can reach Estepona around 14 nautical miles west or push east towards Benalmadena. Longer runs mean an earlier start and more fuel.

When should I book a summer charter?

For July and August dates, reserve several weeks ahead, since the best yachts and weekend slots from Puerto Banus fill quickly. Spring and autumn are more flexible and cheaper.

Yacht hire in Marbella rewards a little planning: choose your marina, match the boat to your group, and book early for summer dates. If you own a suitable vessel on this coast, you can also rent out your boat and earn from the busy Costa del Sol season on the Mediterranean.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
